narrative.intro: Welcome to the Capital Cup final! The stadium is roaring. Score by shooting at the goal with the right capital city — the keeper guards the wrong ones.
narrative.outro: Full time! You lift the Capital Cup as the crowd chants your name. World geography: defeated.
perLevel: First half: Europe. The keeper looks nervous already.
Second half: the whole world is in play. Bring it home!

teaching.text: A capital city is where a country's government sits — its parliament, leaders and ministries. It is NOT always the biggest or most famous city in the country.
Coach's tip: watch out for famous-city traps! Many countries have a superstar city that is NOT the capital — keep your eye on where the government actually sits.

items.prompt: What is the capital of France?
items.explanation: Paris is France's capital — home of its government, parliament and the famous Eiffel Tower.
hints: It is the city of the Eiffel Tower and the river Seine.
The other three are all on or near the south coast — the capital is in the north.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Germany?
items.explanation: Berlin is Germany's capital, famous for the Brandenburg Gate and the story of its once-divided wall.
hints: This city was once split in two by a famous wall.
It is not the banking city (Frankfurt) and not the Oktoberfest city (Munich).

items.prompt: What is the capital of Spain?
items.explanation: Madrid sits right in the centre of Spain and is its capital — Barcelona is bigger in fame, not in government.
hints: It sits almost exactly in the middle of the country.
The famous beach-and-football city in the north-east is the trap here.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Italy?
items.explanation: Rome — the Eternal City — has been Italy's capital since 1871 and was once the heart of an ancient empire.
hints: An ancient empire was ruled from this city two thousand years ago.
Not the fashion city, not the canal city — think Colosseum.

items.prompt: Which of these European cities is NOT a capital?
items.explanation: Lisbon, Vienna and Warsaw are capitals (Portugal, Austria, Poland). Barcelona is huge — but Madrid runs Spain.
hints: Three of these host national governments. One only hosts amazing football.
Portugal, Austria and Poland — match those countries first and see who is left over.

levels.title: Second Half — Capitals of the World

teaching.text: Around the world, several countries deliberately BUILT a new capital city from scratch — to be neutral, central, or planned — instead of using their largest city.
Classic traps ahead: Brazil's capital is not Rio, Australia's is not Sydney, Canada's is not Toronto, and the USA's is not New York. The government city wins the point!

items.prompt: What is the capital of Japan?
items.explanation: Tokyo is Japan's capital and one of the biggest cities in the whole world. Kyoto was the capital long, long ago.
hints: Japan's current capital is also its biggest mega-city.
Careful: one option was the old imperial capital centuries ago — its letters are suspiciously similar.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Egypt?
items.explanation: Cairo, on the Nile, is Egypt's capital and the largest city in the Arab world.
hints: It sits on the Nile, just across the river from the great pyramids.
Not the Mediterranean port and not the city of ancient temples in the south.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Brazil?
items.explanation: Brasília was built brand-new just to be the capital. Rio is the famous one; Brasília is where the leaders work.
hints: This city was planned and built from scratch in the 1950s JUST to be the capital.
It is not the carnival city with the famous statue, and not the giant business city.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Canada?
items.explanation: Ottawa was picked as a fair middle choice between two big rival cities — quieter than Toronto, but the leaders work there.
hints: It was picked as a fair, peaceful middle choice between two regions.
Cross out the hockey-famous giants — the capital is the calmer city on a river border.

items.prompt: What is the capital of Australia?
items.explanation: Sydney and Melbourne both wanted the title, so Australia built a brand-new capital between them in 1913.
hints: Two rival mega-cities argued so much that a NEW city was built between them.
It is not the opera-house city and not the coffee capital — it is the planned one inland.

items.prompt: What is the capital of the United States?
items.explanation: Washington, D.C. is a special district that belongs to no state — built on purpose to host the US government.
hints: It is a special district, not part of any state.
The skyscraper city with the Statue of Liberty is the trap — the capital is further south.
